channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple could launch the iPhone SE 4 as the iPhone 16E, according to rumors
item.description: The handset previously known as the iPhone SE 4 could in fact become the iPhone 16E, according to leaks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 6 ways you’re failing at change management
item.description: In the digital transformation era, organizational change is constant. It’s up to IT leaders to ensure the changes their digital initiatives bring to business workflows are absorbed and acted upon by the users impacted by them.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China hacked Treasury Department earlier this month: Officials
item.description: The bad actor overrode security measures and remotely accessed workstations and "certain unclassified documents" from users.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk throws support behind Johnson for Speaker
item.description: Elon Musk backed Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, as the top Republican in the House attempts to secure support ahead of Friday’s Speaker vote. The tech mogul’s vote of confidence followed President-elect Trump’s “Complete & Total” endorsement of Johnson earlier in the day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: Officials in Rhode Island say cybercriminals who hacked the state's system for health and benefits programs have released at least some of the files to a site on the dark web.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meet the mad scientist of headphones, whose wild designs electrify Reddit
item.description: The standard shape for big, chunky headphones has long been a rainbow with Moon Pies at either end. No rule exists, however, forcing audio companies to keep making them that way. Philip Kaplan, the tech entrepreneur behind Tiny Letter and DistroKid, has been proving as much all year.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk penned an op-ed further expressing support for the far-right German political party Alternative for Germany (AfD). In response, the paper’s commentary editor resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Opinion: Why I’m getting rid of my smartwatch
item.description: Those sleek electronics can distract us from listening to our bodies in favor of chasing digital rewards.
item.dc:creator: Cate Twining-Ward, The Mercury News

item.title: Android 15's new Private Space feature puts an end to app snooping
item.description: Android 15's latest feature, Private Space, is like a private vault helping to keep your personal or sensitive apps away from prying eyes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech investor Cathie Wood on how Trump can boost the U.S. economy
item.description: U.S. tech investor Cathie Wood is calling on Donald Trump’s incoming administration to boost economic growth and policy certainty by backdating promised corporate and personal tax cuts to Jan. 1, 2025, she told Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: How the H1-B visa is causing strife within Trump’s coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in his political movement into public display, previewing the fissures and contradictory views his coalition could bring to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked the Supreme Court to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: DC air defense gets major upgrade
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: An ex-OpenAI exec and futurist talks about AI in 2025 and beyond
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cyberattack hits Japan Airlines, delaying flights for holiday travelers
item.description: Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: YouTube has a new plan to combat clickbait
item.description: Thumbnails play the YouTube equivalent of a movie poster, aiming to draw your attention to click and watch when you have hundreds of videos clogging your recommended content.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Got new electronics for the holidays? Here's what to do first
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy walks through steps he recommends immediately taking after getting new electronics — like phones or TVs — as gifts this holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five big events shaped Microsoft’s 2024
item.description: This was an important year for Microsoft: it added approximately a half-trillion dollars to its market valuation and cemented its lead as the world’s leading generative AI (genAI) company.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation
